headline: Broadcom builds custom chip for OpenAI
detail: Broadcom announced a custom AI chip for OpenAI, framing itself as an Nvidia alternative. This is a double-edged sword for your AVGO position — it shows Broadcom's AI relevance, but it also confirms the hyperscalers are moving away from off-the-shelf silicon, which pressures Broadcom's margins long-term. You're already down 12.45% in your largest position; watch how the market reads this.

headline: Analyst floats Warsh rate-hike greenlight
detail: A market analyst is suggesting the White House may be signaling Fed Chair Warsh to raise rates. Higher rates compress the multiples on every growth and tech name you hold — PLTR, CRWD, AVGO, SMH all get hit. On margin, a rate-driven selloff in your concentrated tech book amplifies losses fast.

headline: $87.6B Iran war spending request
detail: The White House is asking for nearly $88B in supplemental war and farm spending. That's inflationary and adds to deficit pressure, which pushes long-term yields higher. Higher yields hurt tech valuations — this is macro headwind directly aimed at your entire portfolio.

headline: Micron surges 12% on memory crunch
detail: Micron's revenue quadrupled on soaring memory prices, and the stock jumped 12%. This is a positive read-through for the chip sector broadly and for SMH, which you're holding with an 11.32% gain. It won't fix your losing positions, but it gives SMH legs and is the one bright spot in your book today.
